author: Chidera Ibe
name: Megakernel Saturation Study: 5 Triton Fusion Variants Cannot Beat torch.compile at 27M Scale
technique_summary: Novel 5-variant ablation of manual Triton block-level MLP fusion at 27M x 600s x H100. Tests whether hand-written fused kernels can beat torch.compile's automatic fusion. All 5 variants - including an audit-guided best-practices version and the exact PR #1450 architecture that claims +10.5% throughput - land within 0.0008 BPB of each other, all worse than eager. Proves block-level kernel fusion is not a viable lever at this scale.

variants_tested.v1: RMSNorm fused inside K-loop (bf16 inv_rms, in-K scale)
variants_tested.v2: Simpler fusion (matmul + activation only, RMSNorm stays eager)
variants_tested.v4: All 3 audit fixes (epilogue scale, fp32 inv_rms, GROUP_SIZE_M=8 L2 swizzle)
variants_tested.v5: PR #1450 act_grad architecture (save derivative in forward, backward just multiplies)

compliance.non_record_reason: Single-seed systematic ablation for research finding. All 5 variants saturate within 0.0008 BPB noise floor, so more seeds would not change the conclusion.
